Based on the search results, here's information about the `/agents` command slash command in Claude Code:

## The /agents Slash Command

The `/agents` slash command can be used to set up the markdown file structure for creating agents. This command helps you initialize agent configurations in Claude Code.

## Agents vs Slash Commands

While both are powerful features in Claude Code, they serve different purposes:

- Slash commands can be invoked explicitly (you type /command) and can also be auto-invoked by Claude when the command's description matches the task

- Agents are described as "specialized AI assistants that can be invoked to handle specific types of tasks"

## Key Differences

A mechanical benefit of agents is that you can tell Claude to run multiple agents in sequence, which is not possible with slash commands. Agents and slash commands both let you invoke complex behavior with a simple trigger, keeping detailed prompts out of context until needed, though subagents are more like MCP tools where only their description loads into main context on startup, and the full prompt loads in the agent's isolated context.

For more detailed documentation, you can check the official Claude Code documentation on slash commands and the Claude Agent SDK.

content.text:
Based on the search results, the `/agents` command is not a built-in slash command itself. Instead, **agents** are a specialized feature in Claude Code that work alongside slash commands as a way to organize and invoke complex workflows.

## What are Agents in Claude Code?

Agents are specialized AI assistants configured in Claude Code that can be invoked to handle specific types of tasks. They differ from traditional slash commands in several important ways:

**Key Features:**
- Agents can be run sequentially - you can tell Claude to execute multiple agents one after another (not possible with slash commands)
- Both agents and slash commands let you invoke complex behavior with a simple trigger
- They keep detailed prompts out of context until needed
- Agents have isolated contexts where their full prompts load when invoked

**Agents vs Slash Commands:**
- **Slash commands** are invoked explicitly (you type `/command`) and can be auto-invoked when Claude detects the command matches the task
- **Agents** are more specialized assistants that handle specific task categories, functioning similarly to MCP tools in terms of how they load (only description in main context, full prompt in isolated agent context)

## In Your Current Setup

Looking at your configuration, you have custom agents available including `superpowers:code-reviewer`, which is an example of an agent that reviews completed work against project plans and coding standards.

To learn more about creating and configuring agents, you can consult the official documentation on agent configuration and the Claude Agent SDK.

Sources:
- [Slash commands - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands)
- [Claude Code: Moving from Slash Commands to Agents](https://motlin.com/blog/claude-code-slash-commands-to-agents)
- [Slash Commands in the SDK - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/slash-commands)
- [How to Use Claude Code: A Guide to Slash Commands, Agents, Skills, and Plug-Ins](https://www.producttalk.org/how-to-use-claude-code-features/)
